obituaries.name: Gloria Deen Stanton
obituaries.summary: Gloria Deen Stanton April 9, 1931 — May 3, 2026 Waco Gloria Deen Stanton, 94, of Waco, Texas, passed away peacefully Tuesday, March 3, 2026. She lived a long and faithful life marked by humility, devotion to family, and a quiet spirit of generosity that touched all who knew her. A service to celebrate Gloria’s life will be held Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home. Visitation will be at 9:00 a.m., followed by a funeral service at 10:00 a.m. Gloria was born on April 9, 1931, in Baxley, Georgia, to William Grady Deen and Sidney Hutchinson Deen. Growing up in a small town, she graduated from Baxley High School at just 16 years old. Her adventurous spirit led her to

obituaries.name: Nancy Pederson
obituaries.summary: Nancy Pederson October 14, 1942 — March 5, 2026 Nancy Lynn (Parker) Pederson of Waco, passed away Thursday, March 5, 2026 at Baylor Scott & White Hillcrest in Waco. Graveside services will be held 2:00 p.m. Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at White Rock Cemetery near Ross, with David Young officiating. Nancy will lie-in-state from 11:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. Tuesday at Aderhold Funeral Home in West. Nancy was born October 14, 1942 in Center, Texas, the daughter of Sallie (Young) and Heber C. Parker, both of whom preceded her in death. She married James Paul Pederson on April 6, 1968 in Center. They were married 57 years prior to her death. A 1960 graduate of Center High School, Nancy attended Centenary C

obituaries.name: Elizabeth Jane Smallwood
obituaries.summary: Elizabeth Jane Smallwood June 15, 2010 — March 5, 2026 Robinson Elizabeth Jane Smallwood, 15, of Robinson, TX, passed away Thursday, March 5, 2026. A Celebration of Life will be 11am, Wednesday, March 11, at Mighty Wind Worship Center, 1100 Washington Ave, Waco, TX. Visitation will be held one hour before, beginning at 10am, at the church. She was born June 15, 2010 in Houston Texas. Lizzy was diagnosed with Pitt Hopkins Syndrome at just 18 months old, but from the very beginning she proved herself to be a true warrior. She faced every challenge with a strength that inspired everyone who knew her. Through it all, her beautiful smile and joyful laughter never left her. Lizzy was the sunshine

obituaries.name: Cecil Michael George
obituaries.summary: Cecil Michael George March 16, 1949 — March 4, 2026 Waco Cecil Michael George, 76, passed away March 4, 2026, at home. Services will be Tuesday, March 10 at 11:00 at Beacon Hill Baptist Church. Visitation will be at 10:00 that day. Reggie Blake and Danny Dawson will officiate the service. Burial will follow at Waco Memorial Park. Cecil, lovingly known as George, was born March 16, 1949, in San Jose, California. He proudly served in the U.S. Army and was stationed at Fort Hood and during this time, he met his future wife through his buddy, Romie Ball. Cecil and Elaine Sandifer were married September 15, 1973, in Mexia, Texas, and lived their married life in Waco. He retired from Waco ISD and

obituaries.name: Allie Gomez
obituaries.summary: With heavy hearts, we announce the passing of Allie Gomez, who has departed this life on March 4, 2026 after her courageous battle with stage IV uterine and endometrial cancer. She was surrounded by love and will be deeply missed by all who knew her. Allie Gomez was born on July 31,1973 in Waco, Texas to Fidel and Sandra Gomez who she will now be joining in heaven. Throughout her life, she was known for her strength, kindness, and the love she poured into her family and friends. She had a warm spirit and a compassionate heart that touched many lives. Allie was a devoted mother, grandmother, sister, and friend. Her greatest joy in life was her family. She spent her life caring for those she l

obituaries.name: Yasmin Lee Barrientos Obituary March 3, 2026 - Grace Gardens Funeral Home & Crematorium
obituaries.summary: Yasmin Lee Barrientos of Waco tx passed away on 3/03/2026 at age 22. She was born to Ada Priscilla Morin and Oscar Barrientos on 2/05/2004 in Brownsville tx. A private family gathering will be held on 3/07/2026. Yasmin was known for her unique quirky humor and loving heart. She had a passion for reading, music and video games. She will be remembered for her infectious compassionate heart and kind soul. Yasmin is survived by her mother Ada Priscilla Morin , her brother Andrew Barrientos, sister Bella Nino and her loving cats Oliver and Ollie. Of all the special gifts in life however great or small, to have you as my daughter was the greatest gift of all.

obituaries.name: Wanda Marie Ryals
obituaries.summary: Wanda Marie Ryals June 25, 1936 — March 3, 2026 Robinson Wanda Marie (Wilson) Ryals, age 89, of Robinson, Texas, went to Paradise on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. She died peacefully in her sleep, which was always her desire. Visitation will be held on Thursday, March 12 from 6:00-8:00 p.m., at Pecan Grove Funeral Home in Waco. A memorial service will be held on Friday, March 13 at 10:00 a.m. at the New Road Church of Christ in Waco. A private burial will be held at a later date at the Gholson Cemetery. Wanda was born on June 25, 1936 in Beaumont, Texas to the late William Marvin and Dula Lee (White) Wilson. She graduated from South Park High School in Beaumont in 1954. Wanda accepted Jesus Christ

obituaries.name: Maria Celia Perez Chavez
obituaries.summary: Maria Celia Perez Chavez December 4, 1950 — March 3, 2026 Maria Celia Perez Chavez, age 75, of West, passed away Tuesday morning, March 3, 2026 at Ascension Providence Hospital in Waco. A rosary will be recited 5:00 p.m. Monday at Aderhold Funeral Home, followed by visitation until 7:00 p.m. Mass of Christian Burial will be celebrated 11:00 a.m. Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at St. Mary's Catholic Church of the Assumption, with Rev. Timothy Vaverek as celebrant. Burial will follow at St. Mary's Cemetery. Celia was born on December 4, 1950, in Chalchihuites, Mexico, to Florencio and Florencia (Serrato) Chavez Lopez. She began her early education at Vasdespino Catholic School and carried her learned

obituaries.name: Charlotte Anne Phillips
obituaries.summary: Charlotte Phillips was born in Waco, Texas to Ella Clay (Jones) Philips and James Ray Phillips. She was primarily reared by her maternal grandparents, Katherine and James Ratliff in Waco. She attended Waco High School, graduating in 1959. She worked at multiple jobs in the DFW area and in Washington state before returning to Waco. She worked at Graham Embroidery, and later as a draftsman in the construction industry and at the Veterans Administration Hospital. In 1979 she moved to Austin and worked in the architectural field as a construction specifier. Later she was served as a contract manager for the city of Austin. Charlotte met her partner, Margaret Sanders, in 1982 in Austin, and the t

obituaries.name: Donald Howard Antis
obituaries.summary: Donald Howard Antis June 17, 1947 — March 2, 2026 Katy, Texas formerly of Waco, Texas Listen to Obituary Donald “Don” Howard Antis, age 78, passed away on Monday, March 2, 2026, at Caydance Assisted Living in Katy, Texas. Don was the devoted husband of the late Claudia Antis for 55 years. The two met in 1965 while working at the Travelers Insurance Company, beginning a lifetime of partnership. A proud veteran, Don served in the United States Army in Korea from 1968 to 1969. Following his service, he dedicated 40 years to healing others as a Physical Therapist. After tenures at Hillcrest Hospital and Waco Rehab, he fulfilled a professional dream by opening his own practice, Bosque River Physi

obituaries.name: Betty Stasny Belcher
obituaries.summary: Betty Sybil Stasny Belcher was born on April 29, 1924 to Frank J. Stasny and Rosie K. Sirny Stasny in Taylor, Texas. On April 2, 1948 she married RW (Buddy) Belcher in Bryan, Texas. They were married 51 years when her husband passed away on July 4, 1999. She was a homemaker and she enjoyed making her home nice and taking care of her plants. She also enjoyed camping, fishing, and being with family. She was preceded in death by her husband, RW (Buddy) Belcher; parents, Frank J. Stasny and Rosie K. Sirny Stasny; sister, Julia R. Barrington; niece, Doris Jean Driska; and brother-in-law, Willie B. Driska. She is survived by her sister, Ella Marie Driska; nieces, Rosanne Glinski, Theresa Driska, J

obituaries.name: Stefanie Reed
obituaries.summary: Stefanie Reed November 5, 1971 — March 1, 2026 Stefanie Dionne (Walts) Reed, age 54, of West, passed away early Sunday morning, March 1, 2026 at Baylor Scott & White Hillcrest Hospital in Waco. The family will receive visitors from 5:00 to 6:30 p.m. today Monday, March 2, 2026 at the West Church of Christ. A Memorial Service will be held 2:00 p.m. Sunday, March 22, 2026 at West Church of Christ, with Minister Ernie Albrecht officiating. Stefanie was born on November 5, 1971, in Waco, the cherished daughter of Michael Walts and Diane (Neel) Walts Parma. A proud 1990 graduate of West High School, Stefanie furthered her education at McLennan Community College where she was recognized on the Dea

obituaries.name: Sharon Virginia Holmes
obituaries.summary: Sharon Virginia Holmes August 19, 1947 — March 1, 2026 Kosse Sharon Virginia (DuBose) Holmes, 78, passed away on Sunday, March 1, 2026. Funeral services will be 10:00 a.m., Saturday, March 7, at Pecan Grove Funeral Home, with Pastor Robert Nelson officiating. Burial will follow at Valley Mills Cemetery. The family will receive visitors from 5 – 7:00 p.m., Friday, March 6, at the funeral home. Sharon was born in DeRidder, Louisiana, to Aubrey and Pinnice (Whiddon) DuBose. She married Curtis Calvin Holmes on September 7, 1963. She faithfully supported her husband in his calling to serve the Lord, standing beside him with strength and unwavering devotion. She was a prayer warrior that spent hou

obituaries.name: Donald Jon Cann
obituaries.summary: Donald Jon Cann October 31, 1938 — March 1, 2026 Sumter, South Carolina Listen to Obituary The Grand Adventure Begins Lt. Col. Donald J. Cann began his life appropriately on Halloween in 1938 — a day well-suited for a man who would spend 87 years delighting in surprise, mischief, and good stories. On March 1, 2026, he took his final journey while on a road trip — exactly where he would have wanted to be — somewhere between a new destination and a promising local restaurant. He did not so much pass away as set off ahead of us, itinerary in hand. The Boy Who Explored Everything Long before he flew missions or handed out travel spreadsheets, Don was roaming fishing banks, hiking wooded trails,

obituaries.name: James Howard Pitts
obituaries.summary: James Howard Pitts March 12, 1936 — February 28, 2026 Bruceville, Texas Listen to Obituary James Howard "Killer" Pitts of Lorena, Texas passed away peacefully, surrounded by family on Saturday, February 28, 2026. He was born in Dawson, Texas on March 12, 1936. He graduated from Dawson High School and excelled in football, track and baseball. He was an avid golfer and lifelong Baylor Bear football fan. James retired from LeHigh cement company where he was a supervisor for more than thirty years. He was preceded in death by his parents, Howard and Iona Pitts and a sister, Beverly Pitts. He is survived by his wife, Nancy, of 66 years, and his three children, James "Jay" Pitts of Missoula, Monta

obituaries.name: Amy Rene Morris
obituaries.summary: Amy Rene Morris February 13, 1978 - February 28, 2026 Amy Morris February 13, 1978 ~ February 28, 2026 Amy got her wings on February 28, 2026, at 11:22 P.M. She enjoyed fishing, round-track racing, and being around friends. She was a good person who would help anyone. She would put others before her at times. She was preceded in death by her grandparents, James A. and Betty Brown, and her aunt, Cathy Brown. Survivors include her mother, Robin, and stepfather, Russell Burrough; her father, Dalton Morris; a brother, Bo Morris; a sister, Heather Morris; daughters, Cheyenne Murphy and her fiance Jake, Brittney Harger, Cierria Banik and her fiance, Kyle; grandsons, Hunter and Lance Murphy; as wel

obituaries.name: Linda Fae Hineline
obituaries.summary: Linda Fae Hineline July 15, 1944 — February 27, 2026 Waco, Texas Listen to Obituary Linda Fae Hineline, age 81, of Waco, Texas, passed away peacefully on February 27, 2026, in Waco. A celebration of her life will be held at 11:00 a.m. on May 22, 2026, at Central Texas State Veterans Cemetery in Killeen, Texas. Linda was born on July 15, 1944, in Waterloo, Iowa, to James William Norfolk and Helen Fae Thompson Norfolk. From an early age, she found joy in art and reading, and she cherished time spent with her family and beloved pets. Those early passions for creativity and connection remained constant throughout her life. A woman of remarkable strength and versatility, Linda lived a life define

obituaries.name: Daniel Shown
obituaries.summary: Daniel Shown August 23, 1959 — February 27, 2026 Daniel Robert Shown was born on August 23, 1959, to John Ben Shown and Earnestine Yarbrough. Raised in Waco, Texas, Daniel grew up with strong values of faith, family, and service—principles that would guide him throughout his life. As a young man, Daniel was a standout athlete for the La Vega Pirates in Waco. His talent and determination on the gridiron earned him a football scholarship to Cisco Junior College, where he played linebacker for two years. While at Cisco, Daniel obtained an associate degree in Criminal Justice, laying the foundation for the career he had dreamed of since high school. In June 1980, Daniel was accepted into the Tex

obituaries.name: Carol Ann Jortner
obituaries.summary: Carol Ann Jortner November 17, 1939 — February 27, 2026 Waco Carol Ann Jortner, 86, of Waco, passed away Friday, February 27, 2026, in Waco. A graveside service will be held at 1:00 p.m. Wednesday, March 4, at Rodef Sholom Cemetery in Waco. Carol was born November 17, 1939, in New York City to John Effinger and Winifred Lee Effinger. She later graduated with a B.A. from Hillins College. She was an educator during her working career. She was preceded in death by her parents and her husband, Bernard Jortner. Carol is survived by her daughter, Susan Deely of Georgetown; her sons, David Jortner of Hewitt and Adam Jortner of Wolfville, Nova Scotia; and seven grandchildren. The family invites you

obituaries.name: Barbara Helen Caudle
obituaries.summary: Barbara Helen Caudle July 17, 1938 — February 27, 2026 Waco, Texas formerly of Abilene, Texas Listen to Obituary Barbara Helen Caudle, 87, formerly of Abilene, passed away peacefully at Living Springs in Waco, TX. Graveside services will be held Wednesday at 2:00 pm at Cottonwood Cemetery. Born July 17, 1938, in Throckmorton to Horace and Essie Peacock, Helen was a woman of professional excellence and deep family devotion. A skilled administrative assistant, she served at Catalina Foothills Schools, General Dynamics/Lockheed, and Hendrick Hospital. She was preceded in death by her parents, sisters Jean and Joan, and husbands Robert Smith and Dale Caudle. She is survived by her children, Debb

obituaries.name: James W. Harrison
obituaries.summary: James W. Harrison August 23, 1925 — February 26, 2026 West Columbia, Texas James W. Harrison, 100, of West Columbia, Texas, passed away Thursday, February 26, 2026. A private family burial will be held at White Rock Cemetery in Ross. In his own words as recently expressed: "I have had a wonderful life. I was married for 62 years to a beautiful, incredible woman. We were blessed with 3 great and loving children; 5 grandchildren; and 7 great grandchildren. I had a fulfilling career for which I was able to travel all over the world, as project manager for Dow Chemical. I have been greatly blessed in my long life" Thanks for everything, dad. obituaries.name: Pat Wyatt
obituaries.summary: Pat Wyatt February 1, 1955 — February 25, 2026 Patricia Ann (Davis) Wyatt, beloved mother, grandmother, and compassionate friend, age 71, of Axtell , passed away Wednesday evening, February 25, 2026 in Axtell. A Funeral Service will be held 10:00 a.m. Friday, March 6, 2026 at Billington Church in Axtell, with Bro. Kerry Groce officiating. Burial will follow at nearby Billington Cemetery. Pat was born February 1, 1955 in Waco, the daughter of Lewis and Wanda (Brady) Davis. She was a graduate of R.L. Turner High School in Carrollton , Class of 1973, and furthered her education with college courses at McLennan Community College. On February 28, 1976, Pat married the love of her life, Floyd Wyat

obituaries.name: Jerry Lynn Ballard
obituaries.summary: Jerry Lynn Ballard August 23, 1943 — February 25, 2026 Waco Jerry Lynn Ballard, age 82, passed away Wednesday, February 25, 2026, at Providence Hospital in Waco. The funeral service will be held at 12:30 p.m. Tuesday, March 3, 2026, at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home, with Minister Corbin Smith officiating. Burial will follow at Waco Memorial Park. Visitation will begin one hour prior to the service at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home. Born in Mexia, Texas, on August 23, 1943, Jerry grew up in Mexia and attended Mexia High School. obituaries.name: Bettie Jean Reaves
obituaries.summary: Bettie Jean Reaves February 16, 1931 — February 24, 2026 Hewitt Bettie Jean Reaves, 95, of Waco, Texas, passed away peacefully on February 24, 2026, just days after celebrating her birthday. Bettie was born to Sherman and Edith (Mackie) Mills in Waco, Texas. She grew up in South Waco near the 15th Street area and later moved to Rosenthal, Texas, where she attended school before graduating from Waco High School. Bettie was a long-time member of Hewitt First Baptist Church. In 1950, Bettie married Arno Frosch, and together they welcomed two children, David and Sherrie. In 1961, she met and married Virgil “Bobby” Reaves, gaining two more children, Patsy and Mike. Bettie cherished her role as a
